vocabulary_hits.definition: A digital surface that uses light, gradients, quiet space, and low-friction motion cues to feel ambient rather than mechanical.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: Blue-white gradient fields and blurred spherical forms dominate the identity panels.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: A technical or Web3 brand visually lowers friction through atmosphere.
vocabulary_hits.avoid_when: The surface is primarily functional, dense, or dashboard-led.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A repeated sequence of visual gestures that makes the viewer feel they are re-entering the same world.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: Orb, serif wordmark, silence, and soft image texture recur across the gallery.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: Repetition feels ceremonial or world-building rather than decorative.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A layout condition where restraint, large image fields, and sparse copy ask for slower reading.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: The case moves from a short text block into a long sequence of large image panels.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: A brand surface creates confidence by letting images breathe.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A soft emotional read produced by fabric, natural light, flowers, hair, or printed material.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: Merchandise, field photography, and printed type specimens bring touch into the system.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: Digital identity uses physical texture to become more human.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A play-oriented brand surface that avoids childishness through typographic refinement, spacing, and material discipline.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: Serif typography and quiet compositions carry language about play, emotion, and belonging.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: A playful concept is expressed through adult composure.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A perceptual blend where cool palette decisions are warmed by organic imagery, human texture, or affectionate language.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: Blue gradients are paired with flowers, fabric, hair, and soft belonging language.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: A blue-dominant surface avoids feeling clinical.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A call-to-action that feels like leaving a brand world after immersion rather than being pushed through a conversion step.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: The visit-site link appears after the visual sequence and repeats its label across the link area.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: Interaction follows atmosphere instead of interrupting it.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A presentation strategy where atmosphere and emotional rules appear before functional explanation.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: The page foregrounds a sensory identity sequence before sending the viewer to the live product.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: A brand wants the viewer to understand the world before the mechanics.

vocabulary_hits.definition: The use of refined serif type to humanize or soften technical vocabulary.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: Classical serif treatment carries onchain and agentic play language.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: Typography is doing emotional mediation between a technical category and a broader audience.

vocabulary_hits.definition: A sparse surface that feels chosen because the same few forms and materials keep returning.
vocabulary_hits.case_evidence: The system repeatedly uses blue, white, orb forms, serif type, flowers, and tactile applications.
vocabulary_hits.use_when: Minimal elements produce a curated rhythm rather than a blank page.

notes_for_future_review: These terms are candidates only. They are not ingested into prompts or runtime behavior.
Before reuse, normalize overlap with any existing Brand3 perceptual vocabulary.
Keep the language observational and visible-evidence based.
